text: This monograph is a new stage in the exploration of communication official in Poland. The source material for this study comes from 16th- to 18th-century Upper Silesia and consists of the charters of several towns of different European provenance governed by varying types of municipal law. Due to the extensive competence of the town councils, the quantity of primary documentation pertaining to the administration of these towns not rarely falls into two categories: chancellery language used for regulating citizens’ legal, social and property statuses, and court language used for settling conflicts and defending the peace. The present monograph identifies the features of these two types of official communication, with a particular focus on their pragma-stylistic attributes, text types and the written forms of petitions, notices and other types of documents that were current in that era.
headline: Fully Human Written
